chapters.title: Counting & Number Sense
chapters.concept: Counting is like calling your puppies one by one! 🐶 Point and say 1, 2, 3… The LAST number you say tells how many there are.
chapters.tip: Touch each thing once as you count — no skipping, no counting twice!

problems.q: How many stars?
How many apples?
How many balloons?
What comes AFTER 6?
problems.why: Count up from 6: the next number is 7.

problems.q: What comes BEFORE 9?
problems.why: Count back from 9: the number before is 8.

problems.q: What comes BETWEEN 4 and 6?
problems.why: 4 … then 5 … then 6. The middle one is 5.

problems.q: Missing number: 1, 2, _, 4, 5
problems.why: Count in order: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. The missing one is 3.

problems.q: Skip count by 2: 2, 4, 6, _
problems.why: Add 2 each time: 6 + 2 = 8.

problems.q: Skip count by 5: 5, 10, _
problems.why: Add 5 each time: 10 + 5 = 15.

problems.q: Skip count by 10: 10, 20, _
problems.why: Add 10 each time: 20 + 10 = 30.

problems.q: How many fish?
Missing number: 7, 8, _, 10
problems.why: Count in order: 7, 8, 9, 10. The missing one is 9.

problems.q: Count BACKWARD: 5, 4, 3, _
problems.why: Going down one each time: 5, 4, 3, 2.

problems.q: Skip count by 2: 10, 12, 14, _
problems.why: Add 2 each time: 14 + 2 = 16.

chapters.title: Numbers to 100
chapters.concept: Now we play with BIGGER numbers, all the way to 100! Every number has one BEFORE it and one AFTER it. We can also jump by 2s, 5s and 10s.
chapters.tip: For the number AFTER, count one more. For BEFORE, count one back!

problems.q: What comes AFTER 45?
problems.why: Count one more: 45 then 46.

problems.q: What comes BEFORE 67?
problems.why: Count one back: 67 then 66.

problems.q: What comes BETWEEN 34 and 36?
problems.why: 34 … 35 … 36. The middle is 35.

problems.q: What comes AFTER 89?
problems.why: One more than 89 is 90.

problems.q: What comes BEFORE 50?
problems.why: One back from 50 is 49.

problems.q: What comes BETWEEN 78 and 80?
problems.why: 78 … 79 … 80. The middle is 79.

problems.q: What comes AFTER 99?
problems.why: One more than 99 is 100.

problems.q: What comes BEFORE 25?
problems.why: One back from 25 is 24.

problems.q: What comes BETWEEN 55 and 57?
problems.why: 55 … 56 … 57. The middle is 56.

problems.q: What comes AFTER 71?
problems.why: One more than 71 is 72.

problems.q: What comes BEFORE 40?
problems.why: One back from 40 is 39.

problems.q: What comes BETWEEN 91 and 93?
problems.why: 91 … 92 … 93. The middle is 92.

problems.q: What comes AFTER 12?
problems.why: One more than 12 is 13.

problems.q: What comes BEFORE 88?
problems.why: One back from 88 is 87.

problems.q: What comes BETWEEN 63 and 65?
problems.why: 63 … 64 … 65. The middle is 64.

problems.q: Skip count by 5: 5, 10, 15, _
Skip count by 2: 2, 4, 6, _
Skip count by 10: 10, 20, 30, _
Skip count by 3: 3, 6, 9, _
Skip count by 4: 4, 8, 12, _

chapters.title: Compare & Order
chapters.concept: Which pile of treats is bigger? The sign > is a hungry mouth that always bites the BIGGER number! < means less than, and = means both are exactly the same.
chapters.tip: The > mouth always opens toward the bigger number — it wants the most treats! 🐊

problems.q: Which is GREATER: 7 or 4?
problems.why: 7 comes after 4 when counting, so 7 is greater.

problems.q: Which is SMALLER: 3 or 8?
problems.why: 3 comes before 8 when counting, so 3 is smaller.

problems.q: Which sign is correct?  6 ⬜ 9
problems.why: 6 is less than 9, so we use the < sign: 6 < 9.

problems.q: Which sign is correct?  10 ⬜ 5
problems.why: 10 is greater than 5, so we use the > sign: 10 > 5.

problems.q: Which sign is correct?  4 ⬜ 4
problems.why: Both numbers are 4, so they are equal: 4 = 4.

problems.q: Ascending order (going up): which number comes FIRST? 5, 2, 8
problems.why: Ascending starts with the smallest number. 2 is the smallest.

problems.q: Descending order (going down): which number comes FIRST? 3, 9, 6
problems.why: Descending starts with the largest number. 9 is the largest.

problems.q: 🍎🍎🍎🍎 and 🍎🍎 — which group has MORE?
problems.why: 4 apples is more than 2 apples, so the first group has more.

problems.q: Which is the LARGEST? 12, 9, 15
problems.why: 15 is the biggest of 12, 9 and 15.

problems.q: Which is the SMALLEST? 8, 2, 6
problems.why: 2 is the smallest of 8, 2 and 6.

problems.q: Which sign is correct?  3 ⬜ 7
problems.why: 3 is less than 7, so 3 < 7.

problems.q: Arrange going UP (smallest first): 5, 1, 3. Which comes FIRST?
problems.why: Ascending means smallest first, so 1 comes first.

problems.q: Which is GREATER: 56 or 48?
problems.why: 56 > 48, so 56 is greater.

problems.q: Which is SMALLER: 23 or 31?
problems.why: 23 < 31, so 23 is smaller.

problems.q: Which is GREATER: 74 or 69?
problems.why: 74 > 69, so 74 is greater.

problems.q: Which is SMALLER: 82 or 78?
problems.why: 78 < 82, so 78 is smaller.

problems.q: Which is GREATER: 45 or 45?
problems.why: Both are 45, so they are equal — neither is greater.

problems.q: Which is SMALLER: 19 or 27?
problems.why: 19 < 27, so 19 is smaller.

problems.q: Which is GREATER: 65 or 59?
problems.why: 65 > 59, so 65 is greater.

problems.q: Which is SMALLER: 38 or 42?
problems.why: 38 < 42, so 38 is smaller.

problems.q: Which is GREATER: 91 or 87?
problems.why: 91 > 87, so 91 is greater.

problems.q: Which is SMALLER: 50 or 55?
problems.why: 50 < 55, so 50 is smaller.

chapters.title: Ordinal Numbers
chapters.concept: When the puppies race 🏁, who came 1st, 2nd, 3rd? Ordinal numbers tell the POSITION. Counting tells how many; ordinals tell which place!
chapters.tip: 1st = gold 🥇, 2nd = silver 🥈, 3rd = bronze 🥉!

problems.q: 🏁 In a race they finished: 🐶 🐱 🐰. Who is FIRST (1st)?
problems.why: The one in front is in 1st place — the dog.

problems.q: 🏁 Order: 🐶 🐱 🐰. Who is SECOND (2nd)?
problems.why: 2nd means second in line — the cat.

problems.q: 🏁 Order: 🐶 🐱 🐰. Who is THIRD (3rd)?
problems.why: 3rd means third in line — the rabbit.

problems.q: Which one is an ORDINAL (position) number?
problems.why: 3rd shows position. Plain 3 just counts how many.

problems.q: The winner who came 1st gets which medal? 🥇🥈🥉
problems.why: 1st place always gets the gold medal.

problems.q: Count the band: ⚪⚪⚪⚪⚪. Which position is the FIFTH (5th) circle?
problems.why: There are 5 circles, so the 5th is the last one.

problems.q: 🐶🐱🐰🐦 In this line, who is 4th (fourth)?
problems.why: 1st dog, 2nd cat, 3rd rabbit, 4th bird.

problems.q: Which position comes BETWEEN 2nd and 4th?
problems.why: After 2nd comes 3rd, then 4th. So 3rd is between.

problems.q: In a line of 5 puppies, the LAST one is in which position?
problems.why: 5 puppies, so the last one is the 5th.

chapters.title: Even & Odd Numbers
chapters.concept: Can every puppy find a partner? 🐶🐶 EVEN numbers pair up with none left alone. ODD numbers always leave one puppy with no partner!
chapters.tip: Even numbers end in 0, 2, 4, 6, 8. Odd numbers end in 1, 3, 5, 7, 9!

problems.q: Is 4 even or odd?
problems.why: 4 = 2 + 2, it shares equally with none left. So 4 is even.

problems.q: Is 5 even or odd?
problems.why: 5 makes 2 pairs with one left over. So 5 is odd.

problems.q: Is 8 even or odd?
problems.why: 8 makes 4 pairs with none left over. So 8 is even.

problems.q: Is 7 even or odd?
problems.why: 7 leaves one out after pairing. So 7 is odd.

problems.q: Which one is an EVEN number?
problems.why: 6 shares into two equal 3s with none left. 6 is even.

problems.q: Which one is an ODD number?
problems.why: 9 has one left over after pairing. 9 is odd.

problems.q: Is 10 even or odd?
problems.why: 10 makes 5 pairs with none left over. Even!

problems.q: Is 3 even or odd?
problems.why: 3 leaves one out after pairing. Odd!

problems.q: Which one is an EVEN number?
problems.why: 2 splits into 1 and 1 with none left. Even!

chapters.title: Number Bonds & Make 10
chapters.concept: Number bonds are best-friend numbers that hold paws to make a number! 🤝 The friends of 10 are super special — together they make a full ten.
chapters.tip: Friends of 10: 1&9, 2&8, 3&7, 4&6, 5&5. Know these and adding gets EASY!

problems.q: The ten-frame shows 7. How many MORE dots to make 10?
The ten-frame shows 6. How many MORE dots to make 10?
The ten-frame shows 8. How many MORE dots to make 10?
The ten-frame shows 4. How many MORE dots to make 10?

problems.why: 7 needs 3 more to reach 10. 7 + 3 = 10.
6 and 4 are friends of 10. 6 + 4 = 10.

problems.q: Number bond of 5:  4 + ? = 5
problems.why: 4 and 1 make 5. 4 + 1 = 5.

problems.q: Number bond of 5:  2 + ? = 5
problems.why: 2 and 3 make 5. 2 + 3 = 5.

problems.q: Number bond of 8:  5 + ? = 8
problems.why: 5 and 3 make 8. 5 + 3 = 8.
3 and 7 are friends of 10.
9 needs just 1 more to make 10.

problems.q: Number bond of 7:  6 + ? = 7
problems.why: 6 and 1 make 7.

chapters.title: Addition
chapters.concept: Addition is putting groups together — like gathering all the puppies into one big happy pile! ➕ The total is called the SUM.
chapters.tip: Start with the BIGGER number and count on — it's much faster!

chapters.title: Subtraction
chapters.concept: Subtraction is taking away — like puppies gobbling treats! 🦴 Cross out what's gone, then count what's left. That leftover is the DIFFERENCE.
chapters.tip: When you subtract, the number gets SMALLER (unless you take away 0)!

problems.q: Cross out to subtract: 7 − 3 = ?
Cross out to subtract: 8 − 4 = ?
Cross out to subtract: 6 − 2 = ?
Cross out to subtract: 9 − 4 = ?
Cross out to subtract: 10 − 3 = ?
Cross out to subtract: 10 − 8 = ?
7 fish, 4 swim away: 7 − 4 = ?
Cross out to subtract: 9 − 5 = ?
Cross out to subtract: 5 − 2 = ?

chapters.title: Add & Subtract Together
chapters.concept: You're a number-line hopper! 🐶 Hop FORWARD to add and hop BACK to subtract, then land right on the answer. These are problems from your book!
chapters.tip: Adding 10? Just bump the tens digit up by 1: 84 + 10 = 94!

chapters.title: Number Names
chapters.concept: Numbers have names, just like puppies! 🔤 14 is 'fourteen' and 65 is 'sixty-five'. Say the tens first, then the ones.
chapters.tip: Careful: fifTEEN is 15, but fifTY is 50! 'TEEN' means around ten.

problems.q: What is the number name for 14?
problems.why: 14 is ten and four written together: fourteen (10 + 4).

problems.q: What is the number name for 12?
problems.why: 12 is written as twelve.

problems.q: What is the number name for 65?
problems.why: 6 tens make sixty and 5 ones make five: sixty-five.

problems.q: Which number is 'seven'?
problems.why: The word seven means the number 7.

problems.q: Which number is 'twenty'?
problems.why: Twenty means 2 tens, which is 20.

problems.q: What is the number name for 30?
problems.why: 30 is 3 tens, written as thirty.

problems.q: Careful! Which number is 'fifteen'?
problems.why: Fifteen is 15 (ten and five). Fifty is 50 — don't mix them up!

problems.q: What is the number name for 9?
problems.why: 9 is written as nine. (Ninety is 90 and ninth means position.)

chapters.title: Place Value: Tens & Ones
chapters.concept: Every digit has its own home! 🏠 In 25, the 2 lives in the TENS house (worth 20) and the 5 lives in the ONES house. 20 + 5 = 25.
chapters.tip: The left digit is always TENS, the right digit is always ONES!

problems.q: In the number 25, how many TENS are there?
problems.why: The first digit shows the tens. 25 has 2 tens.

problems.q: In the number 25, how many ONES are there?
problems.why: The last digit shows the ones. 25 has 5 ones.

problems.q: Write 25 in expanded form.
problems.why: 2 tens make 20 and 5 ones make 5, so 25 = 20 + 5.

problems.q: In the number 16, how many ONES are there?
problems.why: 16 has 1 ten and 6 ones.

problems.q: I have 7 tens and 2 ones. Which number do I have?
problems.why: 7 tens make 70, plus 2 ones makes 72.

problems.q: What is the BIGGEST two-digit number?
problems.why: The biggest two-digit number is 99 (9 tens and 9 ones).

problems.q: In the number 39, how many TENS are there?
problems.why: 39 has 3 tens and 9 ones.

problems.q: Write 39 in expanded form.
problems.why: 3 tens make 30 and 9 ones make 9, so 39 = 30 + 9.

problems.q: 4 tens and 5 ones make which number?
problems.why: 4 tens = 40, plus 5 ones = 45.

problems.q: What is the digit in the TENS place of 47?
problems.why: 47 = 4 tens and 7 ones, so the tens digit is 4.

problems.q: What is the digit in the ONES place of 83?
problems.why: 83 = 8 tens and 3 ones, so the ones digit is 3.

problems.q: In 67, what does the digit 6 represent?
problems.why: The 6 is in the tens place, so it means 6 tens = 60.

problems.q: What is the digit in the TENS place of 29?
problems.why: 29 = 2 tens and 9 ones, so the tens digit is 2.

problems.q: What is the digit in the ONES place of 54?
problems.why: 54 = 5 tens and 4 ones, so the ones digit is 4.

problems.q: In 81, what does the digit 8 represent?
problems.why: The 8 is in the tens place, so it means 8 tens = 80.

problems.q: What is the digit in the TENS place of 36?
problems.why: 36 = 3 tens and 6 ones, so the tens digit is 3.

problems.q: What is the digit in the ONES place of 92?
problems.why: 92 = 9 tens and 2 ones, so the ones digit is 2.

problems.q: In 75, what does the digit 7 represent?
problems.why: The 7 is in the tens place, so it means 7 tens = 70.

problems.q: What is the digit in the TENS place of 10?
problems.why: 10 = 1 ten and 0 ones, so the tens digit is 1.

chapters.title: Two-Digit Addition
chapters.concept: Now we add BIGGER numbers! Stack them in columns — ones under ones, tens under tens. Add the ONES first, then the TENS. If the ones make 10 or more, carry 1 ten next door!
chapters.tip: Always start with the ONES column. If it reaches 10, carry 1 ten over to the tens!

chapters.title: Two-Digit Subtraction
chapters.concept: Take away BIGGER numbers in columns! Subtract the ONES first, then the TENS. If the top ones digit is too small, BORROW 1 ten — it becomes 10 bigger!
chapters.tip: Can't take the ones away? Borrow 1 ten — the ones digit jumps up by 10!

chapters.title: Times Tables (2, 5, 10)
chapters.concept: A times table is just skip-counting jumps! 🦘 Table of 2: 2,4,6,8… Table of 5: 5,10,15… Table of 10: 10,20,30…
chapters.tip: The table of 10 is the easiest — just add a 0! 4 tens = 40!

problems.why: Add 2 each time: 8 + 2 = 10.
Add 5 each time: 15 + 5 = 20.
Add 10 each time: 30 + 10 = 40.

problems.q: 2 × 3 means three 2s: 2 + 2 + 2 = ?
problems.why: Three 2s added: 2 + 2 + 2 = 6.

problems.q: 5 × 2 means two 5s: 5 + 5 = ?
problems.why: Two 5s added: 5 + 5 = 10.

problems.q: How many fingers on 2 hands? (count by 5)
problems.why: Each hand has 5 fingers: 5 + 5 = 10.

problems.q: Table of 10:  10 × 2 = ?

chapters.title: Shapes
chapters.concept: Shapes are everywhere! 🔺 A triangle has 3 sides, a square has 4 equal sides, a rectangle has 4 sides (2 long, 2 short), and a circle is round with no corners.
chapters.tip: Count the corners to name a flat shape: triangle = 3, square = 4!

problems.q: How many sides does a TRIANGLE have? 🔺
problems.why: A triangle has 3 straight sides and 3 corners.

problems.q: How many sides does a SQUARE have? ⬜
problems.why: A square has 4 equal sides.

problems.q: Which shape is perfectly ROUND with no corners?
problems.why: A circle is round all the way with no corners.

problems.q: Which shape has 4 sides but is long, not square? ▭
problems.why: A rectangle has 2 long sides and 2 short sides.

problems.q: A ball ⚽ is shaped like a…
problems.why: A ball is a solid round shape called a sphere.

problems.q: A dice 🎲 is shaped like a…
problems.why: A dice has 6 flat square faces — that is a cube.

problems.q: How many CORNERS does a triangle have? 🔺
problems.why: A triangle has 3 corners and 3 sides.

problems.q: Which shape has NO corners?
problems.why: A circle is round with no corners.

problems.q: An egg 🥚 is shaped most like an…
problems.why: An egg is a stretched round shape — an oval.

problems.q: How many sides does a TRIANGLE have?
problems.why: A triangle has 3 sides.

problems.q: Which shape has 4 EQUAL sides?
problems.why: A square has 4 sides that are all equal.

problems.q: How many sides does a RECTANGLE have?
problems.why: A rectangle has 4 sides (opposite sides equal).

problems.q: Which round shape has 0 sides and no corners?
problems.why: A circle is perfectly round with no sides or corners.

problems.q: How many sides does a PENTAGON have?
problems.why: A pentagon has 5 sides.

chapters.title: Where Is It? (Position)
chapters.concept: Position words tell us WHERE something is: top/bottom, above/below, inside/outside, left/right, near/far, front/behind. They help us describe and find things!
chapters.tip: Picture it in your head first: is it higher or lower? In or out? In front or behind?

problems.q: 🐦 is up high, 🐟 is down low. Which one is ABOVE?
problems.why: The bird is higher up, so the bird is above the fish.

problems.q: 🐶 is INSIDE the box 📦 and 🐱 is OUTSIDE. Who is INSIDE?
problems.why: The dog is in the box, so the dog is inside.

problems.q: In the row 🐶 🐱, the dog is on the LEFT and the cat on the RIGHT. Who is on the RIGHT?
problems.why: The cat is on the right side of the row.

problems.q: A house 🏠 is close by and a mountain ⛰️ is far away. Which is FAR?
problems.why: The mountain is far away; the house is near.

problems.q: 🚌 is in FRONT and 🚗 is BEHIND. Which one is BEHIND?
problems.why: The car is behind the bus.

problems.q: 🍎 🍌 🍇 — what is in the MIDDLE (between the apple and the grapes)?
problems.why: The banana sits between the apple and the grapes — in the middle.

problems.q: The cat is UNDER the table 🪑. Is the cat ABOVE or BELOW the table?
problems.why: Under means below — the cat is below the table.

chapters.title: Patterns
chapters.concept: Patterns repeat like a puppy's favourite song! 🎵 Spot the part that repeats and you'll always know what comes next.
chapters.tip: Say the pattern out loud — your ears will hear the repeat!

problems.q: 🔴 🔵 🔴 🔵 🔴 _  What comes next?
problems.why: The rule is red, blue, repeating. After red comes blue.

problems.q: 🍎 🍎 🍌 🍎 🍎 🍌 _  What comes next?
problems.why: The rule is apple, apple, banana. The next group starts with apple.

problems.q: ⬆️ ➡️ ⬆️ ➡️ _  What comes next?
problems.why: The rule is up, right, repeating. After right comes up.

problems.q: 1, 2, 1, 2, 1, _  What comes next?
problems.why: The pattern repeats 1, 2. After 1 comes 2.

problems.q: 🐶 🐱 🐱 🐶 🐱 🐱 _  What comes next?
problems.why: The rule is dog, cat, cat. The next group starts with dog.

problems.q: Big, small, big, small, _  What comes next?
problems.why: It keeps changing big, small. After small comes big.

problems.q: 🟥 🟦 🟨 🟥 🟦 🟨 _  What comes next?
problems.why: Rule: red, blue, yellow, repeating. Next is red.

problems.q: 2, 4, 2, 4, 2, _  What comes next?
problems.why: It repeats 2, 4. After 2 comes 4.

problems.q: ⬛ ⬜ ⬛ ⬜ ⬛ _  What comes next?
problems.why: Black, white, repeating. After black comes white.

problems.q: Complete the pattern: 1, 3, 5, _
problems.why: Add 2 each time: 5 + 2 = 7.

problems.q: Complete the pattern: 2, 4, 8, _
problems.why: It DOUBLES each time: 8 × 2 = 16.

problems.q: Complete the pattern: 5, 10, 15, _
problems.why: Add 5 each time: 15 + 5 = 20.

problems.q: Complete the pattern: 10, 20, 40, _
problems.why: It DOUBLES each time: 40 × 2 = 80.

problems.q: Complete the pattern: 6, 9, 12, _
problems.why: Add 3 each time: 12 + 3 = 15.

chapters.title: Measurement
chapters.concept: Measuring lets us compare! 📏 Longer or shorter, heavier or lighter, holds more or less. A big elephant 🐘 next to a tiny ant 🐜!
chapters.tip: No ruler needed — just look and ask: which one is clearly bigger?

problems.q: Which is LONGER: a pencil ✏️ or a bus 🚌?
problems.why: A bus is much longer than a pencil.

problems.q: Which is SHORTER: an ant 🐜 or a giraffe 🦒?
problems.why: An ant is tiny, so it is much shorter.

problems.q: Which is HEAVIER: an elephant 🐘 or a feather 🪶?
problems.why: An elephant is very heavy; a feather is very light.

problems.q: Which is LIGHTER: a balloon 🎈 or a rock 🪨?
problems.why: A balloon is so light it can float.

problems.q: Which holds MORE water: a cup ☕ or a bucket 🪣?
problems.why: A bucket holds much more water than a cup.

problems.q: Which tool do we use to measure TIME?
problems.why: A clock measures time; a ruler measures length.

problems.q: Which is TALLER: a tree 🌳 or a flower 🌼?
problems.why: A tree is much taller than a flower.

problems.q: Which holds LESS water: a spoon 🥄 or a jug 🫙?
problems.why: A spoon holds much less water than a jug.

problems.q: We measure a desk by laying crayons end to end. This tells its…
problems.why: Crayons laid end to end measure how long it is — its length.

chapters.title: Data Handling
chapters.concept: Data handling means sorting information into neat groups so we can count and compare — like sorting puppy toys into baskets! 📊
chapters.tip: A taller bar or a bigger group always means MORE. Just count to compare!

problems.q: Look at the chart. How many apples are there?
problems.why: Count the apple pictures in the row: 4.

problems.q: Which fruit has the MOST?
problems.why: Grapes has 5 — the longest row, so it has the most.

problems.q: How many MORE cats than dogs?
problems.why: 5 cats − 2 dogs = 3 more cats.

problems.q: How many pets are there ALTOGETHER?
problems.why: 3 dogs + 2 cats = 5 pets in all.

problems.q: 🍎🍎🍎 apples and 🍌🍌 bananas. How many apples are there?
problems.why: Count the apples one by one: there are 3.

problems.q: 🍎🍎🍎 apples and 🍌🍌 bananas. Which fruit has MORE?
problems.why: 3 apples is more than 2 bananas.

problems.q: ⭐⭐⭐⭐ stars and 🌙🌙 moons. How many shapes in TOTAL?
problems.why: 4 stars + 2 moons = 6 shapes altogether.

problems.q: 🐶🐶 dogs and 🐱🐱🐱🐱 cats. Which group has FEWER?
problems.why: 2 dogs is fewer than 4 cats.

problems.q: On a bar graph, a TALLER bar means…
problems.why: A taller bar stands for a bigger number — more.

problems.q: 🎈🎈🎈 red and 🎈 blue. How many MORE red balloons than blue?
problems.why: 3 red − 1 blue = 2 more red balloons.

problems.q: ⚽⚽⚽⚽⚽ balls and 🏀🏀 hoops. How many balls?
problems.why: Count the balls one by one: 5.

problems.q: 🍎🍎 apples, 🍌🍌🍌🍌 bananas, 🍇 grapes. Which fruit is the MOST?
problems.why: 4 bananas is the biggest group — the most.

problems.q: 🐶🐶🐶 dogs and 🐱 cat. How many MORE dogs than cats?
problems.why: 3 dogs − 1 cat = 2 more dogs.

chapters.title: Time
chapters.concept: Time tells us WHEN things happen! 🕐 A clock shows the hour, 7 days make a week, and morning → afternoon → night come in order every day.
chapters.tip: 7 days in a week — count on your fingers: Sun, Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Sat!

problems.q: How many days are there in a week?
problems.why: A week has 7 days: Sunday to Saturday.

problems.q: Which day comes right AFTER Monday?
problems.why: The order is Monday, then Tuesday.

problems.q: When do we usually sleep?
problems.why: We usually sleep at night when it is dark.

problems.q: When does the sun rise?
problems.why: The sun rises in the morning to start the day.

problems.q: A clock shows 3 o'clock 🕒. Which number is the short hour hand on?
problems.why: At 3 o'clock the short hour hand points to the 3.

problems.q: What do we use to tell the time?
problems.why: A clock or a watch tells us the time.

problems.q: How many MONTHS are there in a year?
problems.why: A year has 12 months, January to December.

problems.q: Which comes FIRST in the day?
problems.why: The day begins in the morning.

problems.q: Which day comes right AFTER Friday?
problems.why: The order is Friday, then Saturday.

problems.q: What time is it?
What time does the clock show?
What time is it now?
What o'clock is the clock showing?

chapters.title: Word Problems
chapters.concept: A word problem is a tiny story with a hidden math puzzle! 📖 Read it, find the numbers, decide ADD or TAKE AWAY, then solve.
chapters.tip: 'Left', 'ate', 'gave away' → SUBTRACT.  'Altogether', 'in all', 'more' → ADD!

problems.q: Simba 🐶 had 5 bones and ate 2. How many bones are LEFT?
problems.why: He ate some, so we take away: 5 − 2 = 3 bones left.

problems.q: Golu 🐶 has 4 toys. Advaita gives 3 more. How many toys NOW?
problems.why: She gets more, so we add: 4 + 3 = 7 toys.

problems.q: There are 6 puppies. 2 run away. How many STAY?
problems.why: Some leave, so we take away: 6 − 2 = 4 puppies stay.

problems.q: Mother gave 50 rupees. Dog food cost 40 rupees. How much money is LEFT?
problems.why: We spent some, so we take away: ₹50 − ₹40 = ₹10 left.

problems.q: Whity 🐶 ate 3 biscuits in the morning and 4 at night. How many in ALL?
problems.why: We put them together: 3 + 4 = 7 biscuits.

problems.q: There are 8 balloons. 5 pop. How many balloons are LEFT?
problems.why: Some pop, so we take away: 8 − 5 = 3 balloons.

problems.q: Shiba coloured 30 flowers red and 12 yellow. How many flowers ALTOGETHER?
problems.why: 'Altogether' means add: 30 + 12 = 42.

problems.q: Sam bought 35 stickers and gave 5 to Rohit. How many does Sam have NOW?
problems.why: 'Gave' means take away: 35 − 5 = 30 stickers.

problems.q: Ria sold 20 hotdogs and 11 burgers. What is the TOTAL sold?
problems.why: 'Total' means add: 20 + 11 = 31.

problems.q: Siya had 10 cupcakes and shared 4 with Tina. How many are LEFT with Siya?
problems.why: 'Shared/gave' means take away: 10 − 4 = 6 left.

problems.q: Sara has 32 apples and buys 15 more. How many apples NOW?
problems.why: 'Buys more' means add: 32 + 15 = 47.

problems.q: Rahul has 15 apples 🍎 and buys 12 more. How many now?
problems.why: Buys more → add: 15 + 12 = 27 apples.

problems.q: Priya has 28 pencils ✏️ and gives away 9. How many are LEFT?
problems.why: Gives away → subtract: 28 − 9 = 19 pencils.

problems.q: Aarav has 23 books 📚 and buys 17 more. How many now?
problems.why: Buys more → add: 23 + 17 = 40 books.

problems.q: Sneha has 42 candies 🍬 and gives away 15. How many are LEFT?
problems.why: Gives away → subtract: 42 − 15 = 27 candies.

problems.q: Vikram has 19 toys 🧸 and buys 24 more. How many now?
problems.why: Buys more → add: 19 + 24 = 43 toys.

problems.q: Ananya has 35 balloons 🎈 and gives away 18. How many are LEFT?
problems.why: Gives away → subtract: 35 − 18 = 17 balloons.

problems.q: Rahul has 27 apples 🍎 and buys 14 more. How many now?
problems.why: Buys more → add: 27 + 14 = 41 apples.

problems.q: Priya has 50 pencils ✏️ and gives away 23. How many are LEFT?
problems.why: Gives away → subtract: 50 − 23 = 27 pencils.

problems.q: Aarav has 16 books 📚 and buys 29 more. How many now?
problems.why: Buys more → add: 16 + 29 = 45 books.

problems.q: Sneha has 38 candies 🍬 and gives away 12. How many are LEFT?
problems.why: Gives away → subtract: 38 − 12 = 26 candies.

chapters.title: Money & Coins
chapters.concept: Money buys things! In India we use rupees (₹). We ADD when we get or earn money, and SUBTRACT when we spend it — just like Advaita earns 🪙 coins to spend in the Puppy Mall!
chapters.tip: Getting or earning money → ADD. Spending or paying → SUBTRACT. The ₹ sign means rupees!

problems.q: Mother gave ₹50. Dog food cost ₹40. How much money is LEFT?
problems.why: Spending takes away: 50 − 40 = ₹10 left.

problems.q: You have 17 coins. Your brother takes 14 coins. How many coins are LEFT?
problems.why: He takes some away: 17 − 14 = 3 coins left.

problems.q: A toffee costs ₹2 and a biscuit costs ₹5. Which costs MORE?
problems.why: ₹5 is more than ₹2, so the biscuit costs more.

problems.q: You earn ₹10, then earn ₹5 more. How much money ALTOGETHER?
problems.why: Earning adds up: 10 + 5 = ₹15.

problems.q: A pencil costs ₹6. You pay with ₹10. How much change do you get BACK?
problems.why: Change = what you pay minus the cost: 10 − 6 = ₹4 back.

problems.q: Advaita earned 8 🪙 coins, then 2 more for the puppies. How many coins now?
problems.why: Add them up: 8 + 2 = 10 coins for the Puppy Mall!

chapters.title: Revision — Show What You Know!
chapters.concept: Champion time! 🏆 A mix of everything you've learned — counting, adding, taking away, shapes and more. Show how clever you are!
chapters.tip: Read carefully and tap 'Show me how' if you're stuck. Champions never give up!

problems.q: How many puppies?
Which is GREATER: 8 or 6?
problems.why: 8 comes after 6, so 8 is greater.

problems.q: How many sides does a square have? ⬜
problems.why: A square has 4 equal sides.

problems.q: Is 10 even or odd?
problems.why: 10 makes 5 pairs with none left over. It is even.

problems.q: In the number 24, how many TENS?
problems.why: 24 has 2 tens and 4 ones.
7 and 3 make 10.

problems.q: What time is it?
How many MORE dots to make 10?
Look at the chart. Which group has MORE?
problems.why: 5 hearts is more than 3 stars.

problems.q: In the number 47, how many TENS?
problems.why: 47 has 4 tens and 7 ones.

problems.q: A toffee costs ₹2 and a cake costs ₹9. Which costs MORE?
problems.why: ₹9 is more than ₹2, so the cake costs more.
